name: Tres Cantos and Soto de Viñuelas Loop
description: Enjoy an easy 21.2-mile (34.2 km) road cycling route through Cork Oak Park and Tres Cantos Central Park near Tres Cantos.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Tres Cantos are set within the Cuenca Alta del Manzanares Regional Park, a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve, offering varied landscapes. The terrain features gentle undulations and proximity to the foothills of the Sierra de Guadarrama mountains. The city itself is encircled by the "Anillo Verde," an 11-mile (18 km) natural belt, providing urban green spaces and natural corridors. This combination of natural parks and urban green infrastructure creates diverse road cycling opportunities. Best road cycling routes in Tres Cantos The most popular road cycling route is Alto del Cerro de San Pedro – Puerto de Canencia climb loop from Colmenar Viejo, an 80.8 miles (130.1 km) trail that takes 6 hours 44 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain, challenging experienced cyclists. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Tres Cantos complete, an easy 21.2 miles (34.2 km) path. This loop is suitable for various fitness levels, primarily on well-paved surfaces within the city's green areas. Local road cyclists also love the Repsol Picnic Area – Tres Cantos Service Road loop from El Goloso, a 24.0 miles (38.6 km) trail leading through areas with moderate elevation, often completed in about 1 hour 49 minutes. Road cycling around Tres Cantos is defined by the natural features of the Cuenca Alta del Manzanares Regional Park, the "Anillo Verde" green belt, and views towards the Sierra de Guadarrama foothills. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy loops to challenging climbs. The routes in Tres Cantos are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 200 reviews. More than 13,000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Tres Cantos's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Tres Cantos?
A: Tres Cantos offers a wide selection of road cycling routes, with over 50 different tours available. These range from easy loops to challenging climbs, catering to various fitness levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around Tres Cantos?
A: The terrain around Tres Cantos features gentle undulations and proximity to the foothills of the Sierra de Guadarrama mountains. You'll find a mix of well-paved surfaces within urban green areas and more challenging ascents as you venture towards the mountains, all set within the diverse landscapes of the Cuenca Alta del Manzanares Regional Park.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Tres Cantos?
A: Yes, Tres Cantos has several easy road cycling routes perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed ride. An excellent option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e980659190/tres-cantos-complete" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tres Cantos complete&lt;/a&gt; loop, an easy 34.2 km path primarily on well-paved surfaces within the city's green areas.

Q: What options are there for experienced road cyclists seeking a challenge?
A: Experienced road cyclists will find challenging routes with significant elevation gain. A notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14947640" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto del Cerro de San Pedro – Puerto de Canencia climb loop from Colmenar Viejo&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 130.1 km trail with over 2,100 meters of ascent, ideal for testing your endurance.

Q: Are there any notable landmarks or natural features to see along the road cycling routes?
A: Yes, road cycling routes in Tres Cantos often pass by significant natural features and landmarks. You might encounter the medieval &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2555399" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Batán Bridge&lt;/a&gt;, or enjoy views from the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/957559" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valdeliganar Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;. The routes also traverse parts of the Cuenca Alta del Manzanares Regional Park, offering diverse ecosystems and views towards the Sierra de Guadarrama.

Q: Can I find routes with scenic views in Tres Cantos?
A: Absolutely. Many routes offer scenic views, especially those that venture into the Cuenca Alta del Manzanares Regional Park. You can enjoy panoramic vistas of the Sierra de Guadarrama mountains from various points, including the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3117324" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valdelagua Geodetic Vertex&lt;/a&gt;, or cycle through the green belt of the "Anillo Verde" for pleasant urban and natural scenery.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes available in Tres Cantos?
A: Yes, Tres Cantos offers several circular road cycling routes. The "Anillo Verde" (Green Ring) is an 18-kilometer natural belt encircling the city, providing a circular route through urban ecosystems and green spaces. Additionally, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e980659190/tres-cantos-complete" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tres Cantos complete&lt;/a&gt; are designed as loops, bringing you back to your starting point.

Q: Where can I park my car when going road cycling in Tres Cantos?
A: Tres Cantos is well-equipped for cyclists, and you can typically find parking in and around the city center or near major parks like Parque Central, which often serve as convenient starting points for routes. For routes starting further afield, check specific route descriptions for parking recommendations.

Q: Are there family-friendly road cycling routes in Tres Cantos?
A: While many road cycling routes are geared towards fitness, the city's extensive green spaces and dedicated bike lanes, such as those within Parque Central and along the "Anillo Verde," offer safer and more relaxed environments suitable for families. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6344971" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hervidero Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; area also provides a peaceful retreat ideal for families.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in Tres Cantos?
A: The road cycling routes in Tres Cantos are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 200 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, the beautiful natural surroundings of the Cuenca Alta del Manzanares Regional Park, and the well-maintained paths that cater to different skill levels.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by unique natural attractions like waterfalls?
A: Yes, you can find routes that lead to unique natural attractions. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6344971" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hervidero Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, with its small lake, is a beautiful and peaceful spot that can be incorporated into a cycling route, offering a refreshing stop amidst your ride.

Q: Can I find places for a coffee break or refreshments along the routes?
A: Tres Cantos has a strong cycling community, and many routes start or pass through areas with cafes and establishments where you can take a coffee break or grab refreshments. The city center and areas around Parque Central are good places to find options before or after your ride. For longer routes, it's advisable to check the specific route details for potential stops.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Tres Cantos?
A: Tres Cantos offers good cycling conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally provide the most pleasant temperatures for road cycling. Summers can be hot, especially midday, so early morning or late afternoon rides are recommended. Winters are typically mild, allowing for cycling, though some higher elevation routes might be colder.

faqs.question: Is the "Tres Cantos complete" route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route is classified as easy and is designed to be accessible for all fitness levels. It features mostly well-paved surfaces and a modest elevation gain, making it ideal for those new to road cycling or looking for a relaxed ride.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route primarily features well-paved surfaces. Approximately 20.5 km are asphalt and 13.6 km are paved. You'll mostly be cycling on streets (26.2 km) and roads (6.87 km), with a smaller section of cycleway (828 m).

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this route?
faqs.answer: The "Tres Cantos complete" route is approximately 34.2 kilometers long and can typically be completed in about 1 hour and 38 minutes, depending on your pace.

faqs.question: Where does the route start and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The route has a convenient starting point located right next to a parking lot, making access straightforward if you're traveling by car. You can find parking near the Tres Cantos Central Park.

faqs.question: What can I see along the "Tres Cantos complete" route?
faqs.answer: You'll enjoy natural surroundings close to the city. Highlights include cycling through the Cork Oak Park, and passing by the Tres Cantos Central Park, which features the scenic Lake of Central Park.

faqs.question: Is the "Tres Cantos complete" route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: As this is primarily a road cycling route on streets and paved surfaces, it's generally not recommended for dogs, especially off-leash. If you bring a dog, ensure it is safely secured in a bike trailer or carrier, and always check local regulations for parks and public spaces along the route.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to ride this route?
faqs.answer: Given its location near Madrid, the best times to ride are typically during the spring and autumn months when temperatures are milder. Summer can be very hot, and winter might be chilly, though the paved surfaces generally remain rideable year-round.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle the "Tres Cantos complete" route. While parts of the area are within the ZEPA Soto de Viñuelas protected zone, the public roads and parks used for this route are freely accessible.

faqs.question: Are there any intersecting trails or other paths along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route briefly intersects with several local paths. You'll encounter small sections of official trails such as Ruta Verde 7.8 Tres Cantos - El Pardo, Ruta Verde 6.6 Carril Bici de Colmenar, Avenida de Viñuelas, and Avenida de los Encuartes, among others.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or water stops along the route?
faqs.answer: The route passes through Tres Cantos, so you'll find various cafes, restaurants, and shops in the town center where you can stop for refreshments or to refill water bottles. The Tres Cantos Central Park area also offers amenities.

faqs.question: Is this a loop route or an out-and-back?
faqs.answer: The "Tres Cantos complete" is designed as a loop route, meaning you will start and finish at the same location without having to retrace your path.
